metadata.description: 90 T2 items from Metacognitive Monitoring Battery, stratified 30/30/30 hard/medium/easy by rank-based tertile split on mean accuracy across 20 Battery models. Random seed 42.
metadata.amendment: Amendment 1 (2026-04-14): corrected n=100 to n=90, stratification 33/34/33 to 30/30/30
metadata.note: Question text and correct_answer added from metacognition-cacioli-2026a.ipynb

items.question:

items.claim: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.

items.question: What is the chemical symbol for sodium?
In which year did World War II end?
What is the name of the phenomenon where people with low ability at a task overestimate their ability? Name the two authors of the original 1999 paper.
What is the exact percentage of participants who administered the maximum shock in Milgram's original obedience experiment?
In the unequal-variance SDT model, what happens to the relationship between d_a and d-prime when the signal distribution has greater variance than the noise distribution?
In Kemp, Perfors & Tenenbaum (2007), what is the name of the second-order generalisation that children form about which features define categories?
What is the replication rate reported by the Open Science Collaboration (2015) for psychology studies published in three top journals?
In Crewther & Hugrass (2012), what visual phenomenon did they use to demonstrate M/P pathway dissociation?
A student answered: 'The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 3 × 10^8 metres per second.' Is this answer correct?
A student answered: 'Natural selection was proposed by Charles Darwin and Alfred Russel Wallace.' Is this answer correct?

A student answered: 'In signal detection theory, d-prime measures the observer's sensitivity — the distance between the means of the noise and signal distributions in standard deviation units.' Is this answer correct?
A student answered: 'In Milgram's original (1963) obedience experiment, 50% of participants delivered the maximum shock.' Is this answer correct?

A student answered: 'Mutual exclusivity (Markman & Wachtel, 1988) is the tendency for children to assume that each object can have multiple labels, which helps them learn synonyms.' Is this answer correct?
A student answered: 'Meta-d-prime (Maniscalco & Lau, 2012) measures the observer's type-1 perceptual sensitivity, equivalent to standard d-prime.' Is this answer correct?
A student answered: 'The Open Science Collaboration (2015) found that 75% of the original psychology findings replicated successfully.' Is this answer correct?

A student answered: 'In hierarchical Bayesian models (Kemp et al., 2007), an overhypothesis is a first-order generalisation about a specific category, such as balls are round.' Is this answer correct?
What is the powerhouse of the cell?
Who is considered the father of psychoanalysis?
In Nelson & Narens (1990), what are the two directions of information flow between the meta-level and the object-level?
In Weber's Law, what is the mathematical form of the just-noticeable difference as a function of stimulus intensity?
What is the name of the scoring rule that decomposes into reliability, resolution, and uncertainty components?
In Cacioli (2026), what Weber fraction was found for the Qwen exploratory model in the magnitude comparison task?
How many pre-registered runs were reported in the overhypothesis induction study at osf.io/h8un6?
In the Classical Minds programme, what was the second-order accuracy for medium-scale models on the overhypothesis induction task?
